mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production indicate expanding supply capacity and economic growth, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change against 20-day forward SPX returns is negative over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 9% from elevated levels, it signals a definitive improvement in the labor market that lifts consumer confidence and equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE is below 9%, the 60-day SPX return is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean 60-day return (one-sided t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls confirms that consumer demand and job creation are strengthening together, creating a virtuous cycle that drives equity prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: The mean 20-day SPX return when both RSAFS and PAYEMS post positive monthly changes is not statistically different from zero (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Under a zero interest rate policy regime (FEDFUNDS at or below 0.25%), a moderate rise in the CPI signals that deflation risks are receding and nominal growth expectations are improving, supporting equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, the rank correlation between CPIAUCSL monthly change and 20-day forward SPX return is below 0.1 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving residential investment and consumer confidence, acting as a leading indicator that drives short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The weekly rank correlation between HOUST changes and 5-day forward SPX returns is negative or below 0.05 over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 600,000 units signals a sustainable housing market recovery, which historically precedes broader economic expansion and generates positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: After HOUST exceeds 600, the 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean 60-day return (one-sided t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high unemployment and extremely low interest rates create a powerful policy impulse: the accommodative monetary stance eventually boosts job growth and economic activity, lifting equity prices over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: high unemployment with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE>8% and FEDFUNDS<1%, the mean 20-day SPX return is not positive and significantly different from returns in other months (difference-in-means t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth exceeds 100,000 per month, it confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market strengthening regime (positive payroll growth)
failure_condition: When PAYEMS change > 100k, the 20-day forward SPX return hit rate is ≤ 50% or the mean return is not above the unconditional median.
